title: Economic Burden of Salmonella Illness: Policy Implications for Prevention
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness     globally  ,   imposing     a     significant     economic     burden     on     healthcare     systems  ,   food     industries  ,   and     households  . The   costs     include     direct     medical     expenses  ,   productivity     loss  ,   product     recalls  ,   and     reputational     damage     for     food     producers  . In   low  -   and     middle-income     countries  ,   outbreaks     can     disrupt     local     markets     and     threaten     food     security  .   Effective     prevention     strategies  —  such   as   enhanced     surveillance  ,   risk-based     inspection  ,   and     public     education  —are   cost-effective     compared     to     the     economic     losses     caused     by     outbreaks  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     economic     impact     of     Salmonella     illness     and     provides     recommendations     for     policymakers     to     invest   in   preventive     measures  .   Strengthening     regulatory     frameworks     and     building     laboratory     capacity     can     substantially     reduce     both     public     health     risks     and     economic     losses  .
